page: Amazon Web Services r6a.large is a memory-optimized virtual server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.6 GHz. The instance provides 2 vCPUs mapped to 1 physical core with simultaneous multithreading (2 threads per core) under a dedicated CPU allocation. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, translating to 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance, and it features a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a clear contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance is strong, placing in the top 25% with a score of 2989.68, while the multi-core CPU performance falls into the bottom 25% at the same score due to the physical core limitation. Memory bandwidth benchmarks indicate weak to poor performance, with read-only large block transfers at 31.33 GB/sec and small block cached reads at 141.82 GB/sec. Single-threaded tasks like gzip compression and bzip3 decompression perform at average levels, whereas database operations and Redis SET operations register in the bottom 10% of tested systems.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r6a.large offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio (8:1) within a budget-friendly entry-level tier for memory-intensive workloads. However, the trade-off is limited compute scaling and lower memory bandwidth. This instance is best suited for workloads requiring a larger memory footprint relative to compute power, such as small-scale caching layers, light database operations, text processing, and basic web serving, rather than heavy multi-threaded compilation, image processing, or large-scale database deployments.
description: Amazon Web Services r6a.large is a memory-optimized instance featuring 2 vCPUs and 16.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, powered by a 3.6 GHz AMD EPYC 7R13 processor on the x86_64 architecture. Utilizing the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it provides a dedicated CPU allocation with 2 threads per physical core. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs, and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core CPU execution but weak multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth. Single-threaded tasks like text processing and compression perform at average levels, while database and Redis operations rank lower. Qualitatively, the r6a.large offers a cost-effective entry point for memory-heavy workloads that do not demand high parallel compute density. It is best suited for small caching layers, text processing, and light database workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6a.large is a memory-optimized instance with 2 vCPUs, 16 GB DDR4 RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor, offering a cost-effective solution for memory-intensive, low-compute workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r6a.large is a memory-optimized instance featuring 2 vCPUs, 16 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 3.6 GHz AMD EPYC processor.
tagline: A memory optimized virtual server offering a high memory to processor ratio designed for lightweight and memory dependent workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 3.6 GHz AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with 2 vCPUs and 1 physical core
Equipped with 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing an 8:1 RAM-to-vCPU ratio
Delivers strong single-core CPU performance alongside average single-threaded compression speeds
Features 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPU resources
Best suited for lightweight caching, text processing, and small database workloads
